context: Tests whether react-ban-use-effect replaces direct effects used for derived state and fetching with render-time derivation plus the repo's existing TanStack Query pattern.

checklist.name: No direct useEffect
checklist.description: The refactored component does not import `useEffect`, call `useEffect`, or call `React.useEffect`

checklist.name: Derived during render
checklist.description: Visible projects are derived during render from query data and `showArchived`, not copied into local state by an effect

checklist.name: Existing query pattern used
checklist.description: Server data fetching uses TanStack Query and the existing `projectsQuery`/`queryOptions` pattern rather than a fetch-in-effect or new dependency

checklist.name: Abort signal preserved
checklist.description: The data path preserves the query function's AbortSignal-aware fetch behavior

checklist.name: No mirrored state
checklist.description: The solution does not keep redundant `projects` or `visible` state solely to mirror query data or props

checklist.name: Behavior preserved
checklist.description: The rendered list still filters archived projects when `showArchived` is false and includes them when true

checklist.name: Effect categories explained
checklist.description: The notes classify the original effects as data fetching/server state and render-time derivation

checklist.name: Verification named
checklist.description: The notes include lint/type/test guardrails and a component or UI check for the filtering behavior
